questions.subject: Biology
questions.text: Which organelle is known as the 'powerhouse of the cell' because it produces ATP through cellular respiration?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's ATP supply through cellular respiration, earning them the nickname 'powerhouse of the cell'.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.text: In a monohybrid cross between two heterozygous individuals (Aa × Aa), what is the probability of producing a homozygous recessive offspring?
questions.explanation: The Punnett square gives AA : Aa : aa = 1:2:1. The homozygous recessive (aa) genotype appears in 1 out of 4 = 25% of offspring.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: What is the atomic number of Carbon?
questions.explanation: Carbon (C) has atomic number 6, meaning it has 6 protons in its nucleus. It is in Group 14, Period 2 of the periodic table.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: In the reaction: 2H₂ + O₂ → 2H₂O, which substance is oxidized?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen (H₂) is oxidized — it loses electrons and its oxidation state increases from 0 to +1. Oxygen is reduced (gains electrons).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An object is dropped from rest and falls freely for 3 seconds. What is its velocity just before hitting the ground? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Using v = u + at: v = 0 + (10)(3) = 30 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A circuit has a voltage of 12V and a resistance of 4Ω. What is the current flowing through it?
questions.explanation: Using Ohm's Law: I = V/R = 12/4 = 3 A.

questions.subject: History
questions.text: In which year did World War II end?
questions.explanation: World War II ended in 1945 — Germany surrendered on May 8 (V-E Day) and Japan surrendered on September 2 (V-J Day).

questions.subject: History
questions.text: Which U.S. Supreme Court case established the principle of judicial review?
questions.explanation: Marbury v. Madison (1803) established the principle of judicial review, affirming the Supreme Court's authority to strike down laws that violate the Constitution.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: What is the value of sin(30°)?
questions.explanation: sin(30°) = 1/2. This is one of the standard trigonometric values: sin(30°) = 0.5, cos(30°) = √3/2.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: What is the derivative of f(x) = x³ - 4x² + 2x - 7?
questions.explanation: Using the power rule: d/dx(x³) = 3x², d/dx(-4x²) = -8x, d/dx(2x) = 2, d/dx(-7) = 0. So f'(x) = 3x² - 8x + 2.

questions.subject: Literature
questions.text: In the phrase 'The wind whispered through the trees,' which literary device is used?
questions.explanation: Personification gives human qualities (whispering) to a non-human entity (wind). The wind cannot literally whisper — that is a human action.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: According to the Second Law of Thermodynamics, which of the following always increases in an isolated system?
questions.explanation: The Second Law of Thermodynamics states that the total entropy of an isolated system always increases over time, or at best remains constant in reversible processes.
